canonical_definition: Bilingual benchmark assessing long-context understanding through single-hop and multi-hop question answering across 11 datasets, designed to evaluate model performance at varying context lengths.
papers: Less Mature is More Adaptable for Sentence-level Language Modeling
Modeling Uncertainty in Composed Image Retrieval via Probabilistic Embeddings

definitions.text: Bilingual benchmark assessing long-context understanding through single-hop and multi-hop question answering across 11 datasets, designed to evaluate model performance at varying context lengths.
definitions.paper: Modeling Uncertainty in Composed Image Retrieval via Probabilistic Embeddings

relationships.paper: Less Mature is More Adaptable for Sentence-level Language Modeling
relationships.evidence: We use the LV-Eval benchmark, as proposed by (Yuan et al., 2024a), which subjects LLMs to a more challenging evaluation by inserting confusing facts into the context and by replacing keywords and phrases in the context to make sure that LLMs use comprehension of the context, rather than prior knowledge, to answer the questions. (Section 4.2)
